hypothesis: Matched architecture probe on `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` with `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=2`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=3.0e-3` fixed. Final reconstruction formula is `x_hat = U(Vx) + Σ_m π_m c_m + Σ_i α_i^(1) b_i + Σ_j α_j^(2) b_j`, where `U(Vx)` is a static low-rank trunk library, `{c_m}` is a static soft codebook library, and `{b_i}` is a static residual dictionary; only the stage budget split differs from the mainline. This is `扩展兼容` because LUTurbo can export it as trunk lookup + codebook lookup + two sparse residual lookups over fixed libraries. The single question is whether front-loading the sparse budget is intrinsically worthwhile in the low-EF deployable regime, after keeping the whole matched recipe identical.
next_hypotheses: If this asymmetric probe is clearly worse in FVU and runtime metrics, close the family and return to the `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` mainline next round.
If it is similar in FVU with comparable or better throughput, the next single-variable follow-up is `STAGE1_RATIO` tuning within this family rather than opening another new architecture.
If runtime degrades without clear quality gain, treat the extra asymmetry as negative signal even if final FVU is close.
notes_to_memory: Compatibility: `扩展兼容`. Export formula is `x_hat = U(Vx) + Σ_m π_m c_m + Σ_i α_i^(1) b_i + Σ_j α_j^(2) b_j`, so each branch maps to static LUTurbo vector libraries.

This round must pin `K=128`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR=2`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, and `LR=3.0e-3` explicitly; the last three policy rejects were caused by unpinned matched-recipe fields being interpreted as extra variable changes.

Use this probe only to judge architecture worthiness. Check `tokens_per_sec`, total duration, and `final_total_tokens` alongside FVU to see whether any difference comes from better structure or simply slower training.
self_review: This obeys the single-variable rule by changing only `architecture` while explicitly pinning the current mainline recipe fields that previously caused policy rejection. It is the highest-value remaining matched probe because the other feasible low-EF compatible families already have actual EF=2 results, whereas this asymmetric variant has not yet received a valid run. Risk: historical high-EF evidence for asymmetric staging was weak, so this may simply confirm that fixed front-loading is not worth continuing.
summary: Run one valid matched architecture probe on `lowrank_asymmetric_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` with the mainline `K=128`, `EF=2`, `signum`, and `LR=3.0e-3` recipe explicitly pinned. This cleanly answers whether asymmetric stage allocation is worth any further attention in the current LUTurbo-compatible low-EF regime.
